message:
feat(tcp): replace Instant::now() with injectable Time in TcpListener accept storm detection

The accept storm backoff logic in TcpListener used Instant::now() to
track consecutive EWOULDBLOCK events within a time window, making the
exponential backoff behavior impossible to test deterministically.

Changes:
- Add time_getter field to TcpListener
- Add from_std_with_time_getter() constructor for explicit clock injection
- AcceptStormState.last_would_block_at now stores Time instead of Instant
- note_accept_would_block() uses injectable clock for window detection
- Add wall_clock_now() helper matching the project-wide pattern
- Add deterministic test infrastructure (AtomicU64-based test clock)
- Add accept_storm_window_respects_custom_time_getter: verifies
  within-window increment and outside-window reset without sleeping
- Add accept_storm_backoff_escalates_without_sleep: verifies 65
  consecutive would-blocks double the backoff, fully deterministic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
